pi_items.metadata_value: ISO 22301:2019, FEMA Business Continuity Planning Guide
pi_items.verified_value: ISO 22301:2019 is a real ISO standard for Business Continuity Management Systems; FEMA provides real Business Continuity Planning guidance through multiple resources including the Continuity Guidance Circular and continuity plan templates

pi_items.metadata_value: Emergency Management Directors / Business Continuity Planners
pi_items.verified_value: Emergency Management Directors (SOC code 11-9161) and Business Continuity Planners (SOC code 13-1199.04) are both real occupations classified in the U.S. Standard Occupational Classification system

pi_items.source: This is an arbitrary task requirement set by the task creator, not a claim about industry standards. The task requires 12 slides as a specific goal for this training deck exercise.

This is an arbitrary task requirement for flowchart complexity. The verifier checks for slides with at least 8 shape elements (draw:custom-shape, draw:connector, draw:rect, etc.), but this number is task-specific, not a standard.
This is an arbitrary task requirement. While RTO/RPO comparison charts are mentioned in BCP training best practices, requiring at least 1 chart is a specific goal for this task.
This is an arbitrary task requirement for speaker notes coverage. The verifier checks for slides with >25 non-tag characters in notes sections, but requiring 8 slides is task-specific.

pi_items.verified_value: The setup_task.sh creates exactly 6 slides using odfpy: Title, Training Objectives, Business Impact Analysis, Recovery Strategies & Alternate Sites, Emergency Response Process, and BCP Testing & Exercise Schedule

pi_items.metadata_value: RTO and RPO are mentioned in the draft slides (e.g., Tier 1: RTO 4 hours, RPO 1 hour)
pi_items.verified_value: RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective) are real industry terminology defined in ISO 22301:2019. RTO defines the maximum allowable time for resuming operations; RPO defines acceptable data loss during recovery.

pi_items.metadata_value: Hot site, Warm site, Cold site recovery strategies mentioned
pi_items.verified_value: Hot site, warm site, and cold site are real industry terminology for disaster recovery alternate sites. Hot sites are fully operational (recovery within 1 hour), warm sites are partially configured (4 hours), and cold sites have basic infrastructure (24 hours). These are standard designations in business continuity planning.

pi_items.metadata_value: Tabletop Exercise (Quarterly), Functional Exercise (Semi-annual), Full-Scale Drill (Annual) mentioned
pi_items.verified_value: Tabletop exercises, functional exercises, and full-scale drills are real BCP testing methodologies. Tabletop exercises are structured discussions; functional exercises involve actual mobilization and testing of procedures; full-scale exercises simulate full operations with all resources.

pi_items.metadata_value: BIA (Business Impact Analysis) is referenced in the training content
pi_items.verified_value: Business Impact Analysis is a recognized component of business continuity management referenced in ISO 22301:2019 and FEMA guidance. It identifies critical business functions and their dependencies.
pi_items.source: FEMA Continuity Guidance Circular and ISO 22301:2019 documentation

privileged_info_summary: The task references two real standards: ISO 22301:2019 (international Business Continuity Management Systems standard) and FEMA Business Continuity Planning guidance. The occupations mentioned—Emergency Management Directors (SOC 11-9161) and Business Continuity Planners (SOC 13-1199.04)—are real U.S. occupations. The training content includes authentic BCP terminology verified against industry standards: RTO/RPO (Recovery Time/Point Objectives), three site types for disaster recovery (hot/warm/cold), three standard testing methods (tabletop/functional/full-scale exercises), and Business Impact Analysis. The initial 6-slide presentation is synthetic, created programmatically by the setup script. The numeric requirements (12 slides, 8 shapes, 1 chart, 8 notes slides) are task-specific goals, not industry standards.
